round: 9 of the GPU sec10 GEMM-fusion (attention / axis-A) campaign
host: ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580 (48 SMs, 0 compute procs at every fire = uncontended)
pre_registered_target: fused_ms < cublas_stack_ms on >=1 (N,d) shape, with (a) S in registers, (b) softmax row-reduce via warp-shuffle (shfl_sync), (c) P.V consuming P fragments from regs (mma-from-regs), (d) ptxas-clean + numeric PASS
verdict_tier: RED FALSIFIED (warp-shuffle-softmax design) + ORANGE quantified-partial (the surfaced reg-S+smem-reduce variant)
win_distance: shfl reg-S design REGRESSES vs R8 (1.29-1.56x slower than R8 reg-O); even the surfaced smem-reduce reg-S variant is 1.42-2.08x slower than cuBLAS-stack (does NOT cross 1.0x)

probe_correctness_gate.PART_B_matrix_a_map_sm120: IDENTICAL per-lane (elem->(row,col)) map to the accumulator; matrix_a num_elements=16 just DUPLICATES elems 0..7 into 8..15
probe_correctness_gate.KEY_FINDING: the accumulator->matrix_a repack on sm_120 is a PURELY LOCAL register move (pa.x[i]=pa.x[i+8]=acc.x[i] for i in 0..7) -- NO cross-lane warp shuffle is needed; each lane already holds exactly the scores it needs as a matrix_a operand
probe_correctness_gate.PART_C_end_to_end: reg-repack P.V == smem-roundtrip P.V BIT-EXACT (max|O_reg - O_smem| = 0.0) -> repack proven correct

numeric_gate.metric: rel_rowscale <= 1e-2 vs f64 CPU reference (honest per-row-scaled)
numeric_gate.note: rel_rowscale IDENTICAL to R8 reg-O at every shape -> the register-resident S/P path is numerically bit-equivalent to R8's smem path (confirms correctness; the algorithm is right)

occupancy.method: analytic from cudaDeviceProp (regsPerSM=65536, smemPerSM=102400, maxThreads=1536, block=32)
occupancy.binding_constraint: register count (by_reg), NOT smem or warps; both R9 variants exceed R8's 24 and the >=16 target -> occupancy is NOT the regression cause

timed_wall_ms.method: cuEvent 20 warmup + 200 timed median, GPU uncontended (0% util, 0 compute procs verified before every batch)

diagnostic_attribution.question: why does register-resident S/P (which REMOVES R8's smem round-trip) RUN SLOWER than R8?
diagnostic_attribution.controlled_AB: held reg-S + reg-P-repack (the structural moat) constant; swapped ONLY the softmax row-reduce: warp-shuffle (shfl_xor, 6-deep cross-lane chain) vs a tiny 16-float smem scratch reduce.
diagnostic_attribution.result: shfl reduce is ~1.5x SLOWER than smem-scratch reduce at every shape (0.0485 vs 0.0321 @N512 ... 0.392 vs 0.264 @N4096)
diagnostic_attribution.conclusion: THE WARP-SHUFFLE SOFTMAX IS THE REGRESSION CAUSE. The 3-round shfl_xor reduction (x2 for max+sum) inserts a long warp-synchronizing cross-lane dependency chain directly into the per-tile critical path between the QK^T mma and the P.V mma. On the small (16x16) wmma tile the latency is NOT hidden. The proven-correct in-register repack (no shuffle) is fine; it is the SHUFFLE-BASED SOFTMAX that the pre-registered design mandated which is counterproductive at d=64.
diagnostic_attribution.ncu_unavailable: RTX 5070 sm_120 not supported by the installed ncu 12.9 ('Profiling is not supported on device 0') -- attribution is by the controlled A/B swap, not HW counters

surfaced_finding_smemreduce_regS.what: reg-S + reg-P-repack + softmax reduce via a 16-float smem SCRATCH (NOT a full S/P round-trip, NOT warp shuffles)
surfaced_finding_smemreduce_regS.result_vs_R8: matches R8 at N<=2048 and BEATS R8 by 13.2% at N=4096 (0.2645 vs 0.3045 ms, ratio 0.868), reproducible to 0.1% over 3 reps
surfaced_finding_smemreduce_regS.result_vs_cublas: 1.42-2.08x slower (does NOT cross 1.0x; the axis-A win does NOT land)
surfaced_finding_smemreduce_regS.interpretation: the reg-S/reg-P-repack moat DOES help at large N (the per-tile S/P round-trip R8 paid is amortized away), but ONLY when the row reduction stays cheap (smem-scratch, not shuffle). At small N the kernel is launch/occupancy-bound so the moat is invisible.

gap_closure_trajectory.note: R9's PRE-REGISTERED design (warp-shuffle) regresses to 2.1-3.1x. The surfaced smem-reduce variant ties/beats R8 at N=4096 (1.42 vs 1.60) but does not advance the small/mid-N front.

section10_box: [x] UNCHANGED (launch-amort only). The GEMM-fusion (attention / axis-A) axis remains NOT-above-cuBLAS at d=64. No flip.
paper_reason: project.tape paper_gate forbids ORANGE; the RED-falsified design + the surfaced ORANGE variant are both below cuBLAS. The closed-negative (warp-shuffle softmax counterproductive) is a real finding but re-confirms the wall rather than crossing it.
next_round_wedge: the d=64 small-tile (16x16) attention is latency/dependency bound, not bandwidth bound -- warp shuffles lose to smem scratch here. The remaining levers: (1) larger Q tile (BM=32/64, 2-4 warps) so the wmma pipeline has more in-flight work to hide the reduction; (2) cp.async double-buffering of K/V to overlap the next tile's load with the current P.V; (3) the smem-reduce-reg-S variant pushed to BM=64 + multi-warp to break the small-N occupancy floor. NOT another shuffle softmax at 16x16.

invariants.codegen_untouched: compiler/codegen/*.hexa UNTOUCHED (hand-emit / nvcc-lowered CUDA-C; f1/f2 preserved)
invariants.no_spills: 0 spill bytes on sm_80/sm_90/sm_120 for both variants

artifacts.kernel_shfl_regS: fa_regS_v1_oracle.cu (the pre-registered warp-shuffle design; RED falsified)
artifacts.kernel_smemreduce_regS: fa_regS_noshfl_diag.cu (the surfaced ORANGE variant; controlled-A/B reduction swap)
